#Summary1=(human1) In a 2015 episode of his podcast, Cyberpunk Librarian, Daniel Messer, who is set to speak at the Polaris User Group convention, emphasizes that although pirating images online for a library website is made easy by copying them from a browser, this should not be done because it is freebooting others' work without giving them credit and will look bad for the library.
#Summary2=(human2) Host Daniel Messer discusses the dire significance of compensating authors for their works, citing the misleadingly benign nature of appropriating media online for free.
#Text=I do n't know about you , but sooner or later I need a photo .
#Text=As a library web guy , I tend to use a lot of photos .
#Text=Some are for promoting an event .
#Text=Some might be used to liven up some text .
#Text=A photo might be for a post on Facebook or an email campaign through constant contact .
#Text=The problem is you ca n't just use any photo or at least you should n't .

#Text=It 's funny , if you think about it , that there 's a form of piracy totally overlooked by the music industry , the motion picture industry , the software industry , the publishing industry , and any other industry concerned with the , quote , threat of piracy , unquote .
#Text=And that is that you can right click on any image in your web browser and have a copy of it for yourself .
#Text=Very few people seem to be concerned about this .
#Text=I mean , sure , there are workarounds and JavaScript to disable right clicking on a website .
#Text=The thing is , on any web browser , you can get a screenshot plugin to capture the image on the page .
#Text=You can also use the built in screenshot functions in OS Ten , Windows and some distros of Linux .
#Text=Heck , you can go get some really nice free screenshot apps for all the major operating systems .
#Text=Right click is disabled ?
#Text=No problem .
#Text=There are 1,000 other ways to copy that image .

#Text=Copying an image is easy , but if you want to stay on the right side of ethics , and , I suppose , the law , what you need is an image that you 're free to use on your library 's websites without the fear of a cease and desist showing up on your director 's desk .
#Text=Thankfully , you have incredible free options available to you .
#Text=This is Cyberpunk Librarian episode 32 : Picture This !

#Text=My name is Daniel Messer , your friendly neighborhood Cyberpunk Librarian , welcoming you back to the show that is all about exploring the intersection between libraries and technology , and living that high tech , low budget lifestyle .
#Text=So hey , my listeners , hello , hello , and welcome .
#Text=Cyberpunk Librarian is back on the air or on the net or on your podcatcher of choice , and we have some interesting updates to get to before diving into the show .
#Text=I promise I 'll be quick , because no one wants to sit through a bunch of boring housekeeping stuff while waiting for the show to start .

#Text=So hey , I am heading off to the Innovative Users Group , which is also the Polaris Users Group , since Innovative Interfaces Incorporated , sometimes called Triple - I , bought Polaris ILS last year .
#Text=I 'm speaking this year , so it 's been crazy trying to get things together .
#Text=Make sure my talk is right , pack my bag , figure out what a desert cyberpunk needs in Minneapolis to survive , and all of that .

#Text=Looking at the weather report , I suspect I 'm going to need a parka and mukluks , but if you happen to be a listener heading to the IUG PUG convention , whatever you 'd like to call it on April 13th , 2015 , hey , tweet me at Bibrarian and we could maybe meet up , get some drinks , maybe get some dinner , just say hi .
#Text=Whatever .
#Text=But I would love to see any listeners there .
#Text=And hey , the feedback on episode 30 storytime featuring Skylar and Jessica in Intergalactic Librarian was amazing .
#Text=So coming soon from the weirdo that brings you Cyberpunk Librarian will be a new podcast about high adventure in deep space with librarians .
#Text=Intergalactic Librarian will be its own podcast .
#Text=Look for that around the end of April or so .

#Text=The first show will be the same as episode 30 with some edits , but that will not only serve to kick off the new podcast , because after all , it 's nice to begin at the beginning , but it 'll make sure that everything 's working , all of the feeds are doing okay , and that 's fine .
#Text=Also , there is a new look and feel for the Cyberpunk Librarian website .
#Text=I 'm looking to do some more content with a wider scope , and hopefully the new look will showcase that a bit better .
#Text=Have a look if you 're interested .
#Text=cyberpunklibrarian.com .
#Text=And hey , finally , for those of you who do the Facebook thing , dig on the new Cyberpunk Librarian page on Facebook .
#Text=I just created it .
#Text=So we 're kind of just getting started there .
#Text=But facebook.com/cyberpunklibrarian , you 'll find updates , new content , and all kinds of stuff there .
#Text=Check it out , friends and listeners .
#Text=So , okay , housekeeping is over .
#Text=See , I told you I would keep it short .
#Text=Now then , we are going to talk about pictures and putting pictures on the web .
#Text=And specifically , we 're going to talk about putting pictures on your library websites and social media presences and all of that .
#Text=Because while you might use any image that you like on your personal Tumblr or blog , there are reasons you probably should n't do that with the library 's web presences .
#Text=After all , it 's piracy .
#Text=And piracy is bad , okay ?
#Text=It 's freebooting another 's work and not giving them the credit .
#Text=Unless the image specifically says it 's free to use , always assume that it 's not .
#Text=Second , it just looks bad for the library .
#Text=You know , we 're supposed to be protecting and distributing content , not ripping it off for our own websites .
#Text=And while some collection development folks might bristle at this , your websites are part of your collection .
#Text=Just like your collection , your databases , your magazines , your public computers .
#Text=These are all services you offer to your patrons .
#Text=You would n't just pop down to B & N and shoplift a book because a patron needed it .
#Text=Or I hope you would n't .
#Text=Now , if you would , you actually do have a sort of dedication that I admire , and we might discuss that in a later show .
#Text=However , your administrative folks probably wo n't think too highly of that idea , so instead , you typically purchase the item legally and add it to your collection properly .
